summary: Fase 5 Backend testing complete: progressive strike system + public trust score. 31/31 pytest tests PASSED in /app/backend/tests/test_fase5_strikes.py covering severity matrix, side-effect persistence, escalation 1->5 (warning -> minor -> suspension_7d -> suspension_30d -> permanent_ban), pending_strike_penalty_mxn $inc/$dec, lift_expired_suspensions cron, list ordering, validation errors, all admin endpoints, business finance endpoint, public trust-score endpoint and visibility filter (featured/search exclude suspended_until>now & banned, include past suspensions).

minor.issue: When business has no rating (rating=0) the function uses neutral 0.85, but completion_rate is hard-coded to 1.0 when total_bookings==0. This is fine but means a business with zero bookings AND zero rating gets a soft-good 'good' label by default (verified intentional via 'is_provisional=true').

Lift-suspension logic compares biz.suspended_until == strike.suspension_until; if multiple strikes share the same severity and suspension_until ISO timestamp differs by ms, suspension may not be lifted. Edge case only.

Query filter {suspended_until: {$ne: null, $lt: now_iso}} also matches the literal string 'permanent' if it's lexically < now_iso (not currently the case, but fragile). Inner code rechecks for 'permanent', so safe.

critical_code_review_comments: [OK] strikes.issue_strike correctly increments pending_strike_penalty_mxn only for MINOR severity.
[OK] PERMANENT_BAN sets banned=True and status=REJECTED, blocking login + visibility.
[OK] visible_business_filter_now() correctly excludes banned + active suspensions and INCLUDES expired suspensions via $or clause.
[OK] _determine_severity 90d>=5 takes priority over 30d, ensuring permanent_ban can fire even with sparse 30d activity.
[OK] admin_clear_strike correctly recomputes counters using {cleared: {$ne: True}} and refunds pending penalty for unsettled MINOR strikes.
[OK] PUT /api/bookings/{id}/cancel/business calls issue_strike with reason regular_cancellation (>6h) or late_cancellation (<6h) and surfaces strike info in response.refund.strike (verified in code).
[OK] POST /api/admin/disputes/{tx}/resolve outcome=favor_client triggers issue_strike(reason=dispute_lost) AFTER mark_refunded and creates audit log.

Minor: services/strikes.py line ~145-148 update sets status=BusinessStatus.REJECTED (enum object) instead of .value; mongo can store enum but recommend using BusinessStatus.REJECTED.value for consistency with other str fields. Both work due to motor's bson-friendly handling but linting / equality checks elsewhere might fail.

Minor: pending_strike_penalty_mxn could go negative if admin_clear_strike is called twice on the same already-cleared strike with different settle states; current guard (if strike.cleared: return) prevents this. Verified.
test_credentials: Admin zamorachapa50@gmail.com / RainbowLol3133! + TOTP via /app/scripts/get_admin_totp.py; Business testbiz_dashboard@test.com / TestBiz123!
seed_data_creation: Tests insert/cleanup synthetic businesses with id prefix TEST_F5_ for lift_expired_suspensions test. All TEST_F5_ docs and biz_dd14ed093b51 strikes deleted in autouse fixture before/after the module run; business counters (strike_count_30d/90d, pending_strike_penalty_mxn, suspended_until/reason, banned, status) are reset to 0/null/false/'approved'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fase 5 fully validated. Direct service-level tests are used heavily because building real bookings to trigger 5 cancels would be slow. Test business state is mutated during run; cleanup fixture restores status='approved' and zeroes counters. If new tests are added, follow the _reset_business helper pattern. PUT /api/bookings/{id}/cancel/business auto-strike was NOT exercised end-to-end via API (would require seeding bookings + manipulating appointment_date <6h vs >6h) but the code path was inspected and logic confirmed correct. If desired, future iteration can add an integration test that creates a booking via /api/bookings then forces business cancel.
